title: artemisia campestris var. littorea
intro: Artemisia campestris var. littorea, commonly known as Sea Wormwood, is a coastal subspecies of the Artemisia campestris plant, native to salt marshes and coastal areas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it useful in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Sea Wormwood include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe digestive issues, while also being used as an antiseptic and antispasmodic agent. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
